title: Student Financial Aid * Blindness - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Student Financial Aid * Blindness 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals achieve independence and equality by providing information and referral, employment information, literature, scholarship programs, and a blindness-related adaptive equipment store. The Free White Cane Program provides rigid fiberglass canes. 200 E Wells Street Baltimore, MD 21230 Eligibility: -Primarily blind and visually impaired individuals, but sighted persons may also join -Free White Cane Program: Must be blind or visually impaired, must be requesting the cane for personal use, and it must have been more than 6 months since a previous white cane request Hours: Varies by chapter National office: Monday-Friday 5am-2pm Email (541) 653-9153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
